primary_goal: Build authority for UK ecommerce and wholesale automation through high-quality, specific, operator-led articles. Quality beats quantity; avoid keyword spam and thin SEO pages.
site_structure_note: The public article hub is /articles/. Future posts should use /articles/ unless instructed otherwise.

articles.title: The Monday report your ecommerce team should not be building by hand
articles.topic_type: Commercial SEO and operator-led reporting advice
articles.primary_search_intent: A UK ecommerce or wholesale operator wants to reduce manual weekly reporting and understand what should be automated versus reviewed by a person.
articles.authority_value: Builds the reporting automation pillar with practical views on sales, margin, stock cover, channel performance and exception reporting.
articles.duplicate_check: Distinct from the supplier data article and the hire-versus-automation article.
articles.search_console_submission: Not submitted from this runtime; sitemap updated for later Search Console recrawl.

articles.title: Before you hire another ecommerce admin, check the process first
articles.topic_type: Commercial SEO and operator-led decision advice
articles.primary_search_intent: A UK ecommerce or wholesale owner is considering hiring for admin and wants to know whether recurring data work should be automated first.
articles.authority_value: Shows how to separate judgement work from data movement using operator experience.
articles.duplicate_check: Distinct from the supplier price-list article.
articles.search_console_submission: Not submitted from this runtime; sitemap updated for later Search Console recrawl.

articles.title: How to automate supplier price lists for Shopify, Amazon and wholesale channels
articles.primary_search_intent: A UK ecommerce or wholesale operator wants to understand how supplier price-list automation should work before buying software, hiring admin or commissioning a build.
articles.authority_value: Uses Henry Oliver's operator experience and the 8-hour to 2-minute supplier-file proof point without inventing client results.
articles.search_console_submission: Not submitted from this runtime; sitemap updated for later Search Console recrawl.

quality_rules: Write for UK ecommerce and wholesale operators first, not search engines.
Quality beats quantity; two posts per week is a ceiling, not a quota.
No filler, generic automation hype or repeated points.
No keyword spam or pages that exist only to target a phrase.
Do not invent client results, statistics, dates, prices or case studies.
Every article must include a clear decision point, operational example or ROI calculation where relevant.
After the three starter articles, use scheduled publishing or refreshes rather than adding volume for its own sake.
